title: galium spurium subsp. ibicinum
intro: Galium spurium subsp. ibicinum, also known as lesser woodruff, is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as colds, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties. Lesser woodruff can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in herbal remedies to soothe sore throats and calm digestive issues.
